content:
Key Findings

  • Preston City Council spent £205.9M across 46,711 transactions between 2019/20 and 2025/26
  • Total spending increased by £32.0M (238.9%) from 2019 to 2025
  • The largest year-over-year increase was £25.2M (108.7%) from 2022 to 2023
  • The council worked with 2,517 unique suppliers over the 7-year period
  • Spending peaked at £48.4M in 2023 before dropping by £3.957M (8.2%) in 2024

Spending Growth Over Seven Years

Preston City Council's spending has seen significant growth over the past seven years, increasing by £32.0M (238.9%) from 2019 to 2025. The largest year-over-year increase occurred between 2022 and 2023, with spending rising by £25.2M (108.7%). This substantial growth suggests a period of increased investment or expenditure during this time.

The council's spending peaked in 2023 at £48.4M, followed by a notable decrease of £3.957M (8.2%) in 2024. This drop could indicate a shift in priorities, cost-saving measures, or a reduction in specific projects or services. The spending in 2025 saw a slight increase of £943K (2.1%) compared to the previous year, stabilising at £45.4M.

Supplier Retention and Churn

With 2,517 unique suppliers over seven years, Preston City Council has a diverse range of vendors. The high number of suppliers suggests a strategy that values competition and variety in procurement. However, it also raises questions about supplier retention and the potential benefits of long-term partnerships versus one-time contracts.

Analysing the data further could reveal patterns in supplier retention. For instance, identifying the top suppliers and their contract durations could provide insights into the council's procurement strategies. Understanding whether the council favours long-term contracts with a few key suppliers or prefers a more diverse range of vendors could offer valuable context for future spending decisions.

Year-Over-Year Spending Comparison

The following table provides a detailed look at the year-over-year spending changes, highlighting the significant increases and decreases over the seven-year period.

Year Spending (£) Change (£) Change (%)
2019 13.4M - -
2020 15.4M +2.0M +14.7%
2021 15.8M +396K +2.6%
2022 23.2M +7.4M +47.1%
2023 48.4M +25.2M +108.7%
2024 44.4M -3.957M -8.2%
2025 45.4M +943K +2.1%

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Preston City Council about the top suppliers and their contract durations
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about procurement strategies and spending decisions
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see detailed information about supplier contracts
  • Write to your local councillor about the importance of transparency in supplier retention and procurement practices
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse trends over the past seven years
This article is based on Preston City Council's published spending data covering 2019/20 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
